panel: coupling v6 power delta -- pre-freeze panel #8

lens_reviews.lens: coupled-constants: silent constant breakage under the R5.6 v6 delta (selection widening 48->112 items, decision bar 0.0152 -> 0.0076)

findings.title: INJECTION_GRID is in units of MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE but the estimator pool noise is absolute -- halving the bar makes the TERMINAL estimator VOID near-certain, and falsifies the grid top's own stated derivation
findings.claim: The estimator-admissibility gate stands in front of ALL FIVE claim-bearing branches (coupling_confirm_v4.py:808-822) and R5.3 declares its VOID TERMINAL for the prereg. Its only gating sensitivity leg is ESTIMATOR_MDE80 = smallest k on INJECTION_GRID reaching 0.80 recovery, maximised over the full pool and 5 LOO replicates (coupling_confirm_v4.py:584-594, 660-670). INJECTION_GRID is expressed as MULTIPLES of MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE (line 137). The pool's slope dispersion is an absolute quantity that does NOT scale with the bar. Halving the bar therefore doubles pool_SD/bar and shifts the recovery curve right, while the grid top stays at 8 -- so the reachable injection range halves in absolute terms exactly when more range is needed. Under the widening alone the gate PASSES; under the widening plus the halving it VOIDs.
findings.evidence: Projected pool SD = 3.83x the within-seed slope SE (_estimator_noise_anchor.json within_seed_paired_noise.pool_overstatement_factor). At 112 items the projected within SE is 0.01002 (_estimator_noise_anchor.json projection.rows[1]) -> pool SD 0.0384. Running the shipped draw rule (_pseudo_rates / _frozen_rule_rates, coupling_confirm_v4.py:557-594) on the realized 10-unit pool rescaled to that SD: bar 0.0152 -> pool SD = 2.53x bar, MDE80 = 4.0, full and all 5 LOO replicates return a value -> gate PASSES. bar 0.0076 -> pool SD = 5.05x bar, recovery tops out at 0.750 at the grid top k=8; full=None, loo0=loo1=loo2=None -> `any(m is None for m in reps.values())` fires (line 668) -> VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive, TERMINAL. Sweep: MDE80 exists on the grid only while pool SD is at or below ~4x bar (3.5x->6.0, 4.0x->8.0, 5.0x->None). Separately, the grid top's written justification at coupling_confirm_v4.py:140-144 ('8x bar = 0.1216, a span-scale effect of 0.7296 ... consuming 73% of the aggregate's full range') becomes 8x0.0076 = 0.0608, span-scale 0.3648 = 36.5% of the range -- the stated derivation is false under the delta, and the maximum expressible MDE80 (0.0608, span 0.365) drops BELOW the price of interest 0.0909, so a bounded null can no longer be bounded at the price-of-interest scale at all.
findings.fix: Do not leave INJECTION_GRID denominated in MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE. Either (a) re-express the grid in ABSOLUTE slope units, deriving the top from the argument the comment actually makes (a span-scale effect consuming ~73% of the aggregate's 1.0 range, i.e. top = 0.1216 absolute, which under bar 0.0076 is k=16), or (b) denominate it in multiples of the PRICE-OF-INTEREST slope (0.0152) which is a fixed physical quantity, and keep the decision bar as a separate constant. Then recompute MDE80 on the projected pool and show the gate passes BEFORE freezing. Also restate the grid-top insensitivity derivation in the prereg with the new arithmetic.

findings.title: Delta 1 is a no-op in code: select_disjoint already selects EVERY qualifying sub-task, so 112 items is unreachable without lowering DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN -- an inherited threshold the prereg forbids lowering
findings.claim: R5.6 item 1 asserts 'MIN_DISJOINT = 3 (48 items) is a floor being used as a target' and that selecting all 7 'costs decode time only'. That is factually wrong about the harness. `select_disjoint` returns EVERY GEN_DISJOINT_POOL sub-task clearing the floor; `need` only sets the ok flag. MIN_DISJOINT is used solely as `need` (line 1042) and as a preflight count check (line 220). The harness already selects everything that qualifies, so there is no change that widens the selection except lowering DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN = 0.90 below the base scores of the failing sub-tasks -- and the prereg states 'It does not lower any inherited threshold'. Lowering it also makes CLEAN_SUBTASK_FLOOR = 0.70 the binding selection floor, a constant derived to be a slack clean-admissibility check under a 0.90 selection, not a selection rule.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_gen.py:369-374 -- `survivors = sorted([n for n in GEN_DISJOINT_POOL if clean_scores.get(n, 0.0) >= floor]); return survivors, bool(len(survivors) >= need)`. Callers: coupling_confirm_v4.py:1042 and :1085, both `floor=DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN, need=MIN_DISJOINT`. The realized item count is therefore data-determined at --calibrate (post-freeze, on GPU) and is NOT 112 unless all 7 sub-tasks clear 0.90. The harness's own illustrative base in the selftest (capability_battery_gen.py:572-574: ORTH_FIRST 1.0, ORTH_LAST 0.9375, CONTAINS 0.875, ANTONYM 1.0, ALPHA 0.8125, PLURAL 0.9375, SEQ 1.0) yields 5 survivors / 80 items at floor 0.90, not 7 / 112. Every operating point in the R5.6 table is conditioned on 112 items; none of them is a number the design can pre-commit to. Note also that battery_guard's floors (CLEAN_AGG_FLOOR 0.80, CLEAN_SUBTASK_FLOOR 0.70, capability_battery_gen.py:381-386) are strictly weaker than a 0.90 selection and are therefore decorative today -- they become load-bearing only in the floor-lowering implementation.
findings.fix: State the delta as what it actually is. If the intent is a guaranteed 112 items, that means raising MIN_DISJOINT to 7 so a short selection VOIDs at preflight rather than silently running a smaller battery -- and accepting a data-dependent preflight VOID. If the intent is to lower DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN, say so explicitly, get the inherited-threshold clause amended, and re-derive CLEAN_SUBTASK_FLOOR as the new binding selection floor. Either way, recompute the R5.6 table as a function of the realized k, not at a fixed 112.

findings.title: The SE 0.0153 -> 0.0100 projection uses the wrong variance model: the aggregate is a mean of SUB-TASK means, so widening with the weakest sub-tasks can RAISE the SE, not cut it 1.53x
findings.claim: Both deltas are sold as 'jointly necessary' on a single number: per-seed slope SE 0.0153 -> 0.0100 (1.53x). That figure comes from sqrt(p(1-p)/n_items) with a uniform p = 0.90. But `aggregate` is the unweighted mean of sub-task ACCURACIES, so Var(agg) = (1/k^2) * sum_i p_i(1-p_i)/16, which equals p(1-p)/n_items ONLY when the p_i are homogeneous. Widening can only add the WEAKEST qualifying sub-tasks -- the strong ones are already selected (CC-2) -- which is precisely the regime where adding a sub-task raises the aggregate variance. The delta's precision premise is therefore unearned and can point the wrong way.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_gen.py:377-378 -- `aggregate(scores, selected) = sum(scores[n] for n in selected) / len(selected)`. Computed on the harness's own illustrative base (capability_battery_gen.py:572-574): floor-0.90 selection = 5 sub-tasks / 80 items, TRUE aggregate SE = sqrt((0 + .0586/16 + 0 + .0586/16 + 0)/25) = 0.0171. Admitting all 7 (adding CONTAINS 0.875 and ALPHA 0.8125): SE = sqrt(.023681/49) = 0.0220 -- 1.29x WORSE, not 1.53x better. Under a plausible floor-lowered mix of 3 strong (0.95) + 4 weak (0.72) the true SE is 0.0348 against the projected 0.0283 (slope SE 0.0123 = 1.62x bar, not the projected 0.66x). The uniform-p projection in _estimator_noise_anchor.json projection.rows and estimator_power_prefreeze.py:150-155 (`se_agg = sqrt(CLEAN_FLOOR*(1-CLEAN_FLOOR)/n_items)`) hard-codes that homogeneity assumption and never checks it.
findings.fix: Replace the uniform-p projection with the correct heterogeneous form Var = (1/k^2)*sum_i p_i(1-p_i)/16 everywhere it drives a power number (estimator_power_prefreeze.py CONFIGS loop, estimator_noise_anchor.py section 7). Then make the widening decision CONDITIONAL on the realized clean per-sub-task scores: only admit a sub-task if its per-item variance is below the current selected average, i.e. only if it actually lowers Var(agg). That rule is outcome-blind (base-only, treatment-blind) and can be pre-committed.

findings.title: FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX = 0.0722 loosens under the widening AND its load-bearing disclosure inverts under the halving -- a format channel is now permitted to exceed the entire effect COUPLED asserts
findings.claim: The hazard named in the brief is real, and it has a second face the brief does not name. (i) Widening: 0.0722 is documented as 'one per-checkpoint aggregate SE at minimum selection' = sqrt(0.25/48). At 112 items one SE is sqrt(0.25/112) = 0.0472, so the same absolute bar becomes 1.53 SEs (2.55 SEs at the 0.90 floor SE of 0.0283). The guard silently loosens by exactly sqrt(112/48). (ii) Halving: the prereg's own disclosure -- '0.0722 is 0.7938 of the 0.0909 minimum price of interest, so a format channel nearly four-fifths the size of the effect being priced can pass' -- becomes false. v6 COUPLED asserts a span-scale effect of 6 x 0.0076 = 0.0456, so 0.0722 is 1.58x that effect. The clean-base format channel would be permitted to be LARGER than the whole effect the verdict asserts. A verdict does ride on it: fi_ok gates selmeta['ok'], which preflight enforces (line 217-222) -> VOID_COUPLING__battery_guard_failed.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:123 (`FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX = 0.0722  # PREREG: one per-checkpoint aggregate SE at minimum selection`), :758 ('at the minimum selection (3 sub-tasks x 16 = 48 items) has exactly the SE the bar is'), :1048-1050 (fi_ok -> sel['ok']), :217-222 (preflight enforces ok). PREREG:249-254 carries the 0.7938 disclosure. Arithmetic: sqrt(0.25/48)=0.0722 (1.00 SE); sqrt(0.25/112)=0.0472 (1.53 SE); sqrt(0.09/112)=0.0283 (2.55 SE). 0.0722/0.0909 = 0.7943; 0.0722/(6*0.0076) = 1.5833. The receipt compounds it: coupling_confirm_v4.py:1062 hardcodes `round(FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX / 0.0909, 4)`, so `format_invariance_max_fraction_of_price` would keep printing 0.7943 under the halved bar -- a receipt field that silently states the pre-delta number.
findings.fix: Re-derive the bar from the REALIZED selection at --calibrate: FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX = sqrt(0.25/(16*len(survivors))), computed in run_calibrate rather than frozen as a scalar, and record both the derived value and len(survivors) in the selection receipt. Replace the hardcoded 0.0909 divisor at line 1062 with `(RANK_SPAN[1]-RANK_SPAN[0]) * MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE` so the fraction-of-effect disclosure tracks the bar. Restate the PREREG:249-254 sentence with the new ratio before freeze.

findings.title: FI_DOSE_FRACTION x MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE: the FI downgrade bar halves (2x) while its own noise only falls 1.53x, and the FI slope is a TWO-point fit of a folded statistic -- false downgrades rise ~4-10x
findings.claim: Answering (b) directly: the resulting guard is STRICTER, so the direction is conservative (it kills COUPLED, never manufactures it) -- not fatal. But it interacts badly with the noise and it eats the sensitivity the delta is bought for. The FI slope is fit over span [2,8] on only TWO points -- the rank-2 pin and the rank-8 crossing -- because the third pre-committed FI checkpoint (the final) sits at rank 24, outside the span. So slope = (FI_crossing - FI_pin)/6 where FI is an ABSOLUTE delta, i.e. a folded |N(mu, sqrt2*se_agg)| with a strictly positive mean even under zero true drift, while FI_pin is a single frozen draw at the untrained base. The null distribution of the FI slope is therefore positively biased, and halving the bar to 0.0038 doubles the false-flag rate while widening only shrinks the noise by 1.53x.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:718-751 (`bar = FI_DOSE_FRACTION * MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE`; points filtered by `lo <= erased_rank <= hi`), :935 and :951-953 (FI subsample = step-0 pin, first checkpoint with cur_rank >= RANK_SPAN[1], and :982 the final at rank 24 -> outside [2,8]). Simulated false-downgrade rate (strict majority of 5 seeds flagged) under ZERO true dose-graded drift, FI ~ |N(mu, sqrt2*se_agg)|: at 112 items, mu=0: 0.001 (bar 0.0076) -> 0.038 (bar 0.0038); mu=0.05: 0.011 -> 0.108. At 48 items, mu=0: 0.016 -> 0.095. So the v6 FI guard is a ~4-11% random tax on the COUPLED branch, which drags the R5.6 headline 0.880 down to ~0.78-0.85 -- below the 0.80 the delta is sold on, before the subtractive guard is even applied. Secondary: the sole --dry fixture for this branch (coupling_confirm_v4.py:1323, `fi_map={2: 0.01, 8: 0.07}` -> FI slope 0.01) passes under BOTH bars, so the regression suite cannot detect the bar change at all.
findings.fix: Do NOT re-derive the FI downgrade bar from the halved decision bar. Pin it at its absolute v4 value 0.0076 and restate its provenance as 'half the PRICE-OF-INTEREST slope' (which is what it was), decoupling it from MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE -- one line: `FI_DOSE_BAR = 0.5 * PRICE_OF_INTEREST / (RANK_SPAN[1]-RANK_SPAN[0])`. Additionally, either add a third in-span FI checkpoint or state on the receipt that the FI slope is a 2-point fit of a folded statistic with a positive null mean. Add a --dry fixture with an FI slope between 0.0038 and 0.0076 so the bar's value is regression-pinned.

findings.title: The FI downgrade bar appears on NO receipt for any non-COUPLED run: FI_DOSE_FRACTION is missing from _thresholds() and trained_fi_dose_graded is only called inside the coupled branch
findings.claim: Answering (e): a guard constant that silently rescales under the delta (CC-5) is not on the grounding surface. `_thresholds()` -- the receipt's constants block, which a verifier uses to reconstruct every bar -- omits FI_DOSE_FRACTION and the derived FI bar entirely. The only place the value is disclosed is `trained_fi_dose_graded`'s own return (`fraction_of_bar`, `bar`), and that function is invoked ONLY inside the `if dr['coupled']:` branch. So on every PARTIAL, bounded-null, style-downgrade or VOID run -- which is every anticipated outcome -- the receipt records neither the FI downgrade bar nor the fact that it changed with the delta.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:840-858 `_thresholds()` enumerates survival, clean floors, min_disjoint, min_effect_slope, rank_span, stat_alpha, k_perm, auroc_floor, min_gate_seeds_replicating, max_fire, destruction_knowledge_max, the four count floors, format_invariance_max, and the five estimator constants -- FI_DOSE_FRACTION is absent. coupling_confirm_v4.py:829 `fi_dg = trained_fi_dose_graded(...)` sits inside the `if dr['coupled']:` block opened at line 812; `dr['trained_fi_dose_graded']` is set only at line 830. Contrast `trained_fi_summary(curves)` at line 1156, which IS unconditional but reports raw abs_deltas with no bar.
findings.fix: Add `"fi_dose_fraction": FI_DOSE_FRACTION` and `"fi_dose_bar": round(FI_DOSE_FRACTION * MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE, 6)` to `_thresholds()`, and compute `trained_fi_dose_graded` unconditionally (attach to `dr` before the branch, gate only the DOWNGRADE on `dr['coupled']`) so the FI slopes and the bar are on every receipt regardless of verdict.

findings.title: The two pre-freeze receipt scripts hardcode the v4 bar and the 48-item selection, so the delta's own provenance artifacts would go stale on freeze
findings.claim: Answering the provenance half of the mandate: `estimator_power_prefreeze.py` and `estimator_noise_anchor.py` are the artifacts R5.4/R5.5 cite as the delta's evidence base, and both hardcode MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE = 0.0152, SE_AGG_PREREG = 0.0722 and N_ITEMS_MIN = 48 rather than importing them from the harness. If the v6 delta is frozen, `_estimator_power_prefreeze.json` and `_estimator_noise_anchor.json` continue to describe the v4 design while the prereg cites them as the v6 justification. This is hygiene, not a verdict defect, but it is exactly the provenance-drift class this arc exists to prevent.
findings.evidence: estimator_power_prefreeze.py:31-38 (`PRICE_OF_INTEREST = 0.0909`, `MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE = 0.0152`, `SE_AGG_PREREG = 0.0722`, `N_ITEMS_MIN = 48`, `CLEAN_FLOOR = 0.90`) and estimator_noise_anchor.py:21-24 (`MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE = 0.0152`, `SE_AGG_PREREG = 0.0722`). Neither imports from coupling_confirm_v4.py. The R5.6 operating-point table reproduces exactly under these constants (I re-simulated: 112/0.0152 -> sens 0.499 fpr 0.002; 112/0.0114 -> 0.750/0.013; 112/0.0076 -> 0.878/0.042; 112/0.0061 -> 0.886/0.056; 48/0.0076 -> 0.623/0.063; 48/0.0152 -> 0.464/0.021), so the table is arithmetically sound ON ITS OWN noise model -- the defect is that the model is the within-seed one while the gate that must pass uses the 3.83x-larger cross-seed pool (CC-1).
findings.fix: Have both scripts import the constants from coupling_confirm_v4 rather than restating them, re-run both after any bar or selection change, and record the emitted JSON SHAs in the prereg beside the freeze SHA.

lens_reviews.summary:
The R5.6 v6 delta does not survive the coupled-constants lens. Three findings are fatal and they are independent of each other.

The single most damaging one is CC-1: INJECTION_GRID is denominated in multiples of MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE, but the estimator pool's dispersion is an absolute quantity that does not scale with the decision bar. Halving the bar doubles pool_SD/bar and halves the grid's absolute reach at the same time. Running the shipped draw rule on the realized pool rescaled to 112 items: at bar 0.0152 the gate passes with MDE80 = 4.0; at bar 0.0076 recovery tops out at 0.750 at the grid top and the full pool plus three of five LOO replicates return None, which fires VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive -- a VOID that R5.3 declares TERMINAL for the prereg. The delta as written converts the most likely outcome from PARTIAL into the end of attempt 4. The grid top's own written derivation ("a span-scale effect of 0.7296 ... 73% of the aggregate's full range") also becomes false: it is 36.5% under the halved bar, and the maximum expressible MDE80 drops below the price of interest, so a bounded null could no longer be bounded at the scale the paper asks about.

CC-2 and CC-3 dismantle the delta's precision premise. select_disjoint (capability_battery_gen.py:369-374) already returns every sub-task clearing the floor; MIN_DISJOINT is only the `need` count. "Select every qualifying disjoint sub-task instead of the MIN_DISJOINT floor of 3" describes behaviour the harness already has, so 112 items is unreachable except by lowering DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN = 0.90 -- an inherited threshold the prereg forbids lowering, and whose removal makes CLEAN_SUBTASK_FLOOR = 0.70 the binding selection rule. And the 1.53x SE gain is the wrong variance model: aggregate() is a mean of sub-task means, so Var = (1/k^2)*sum p_i(1-p_i)/16, which only equals p(1-p)/n under homogeneous p. Widening can only add the weakest qualifying sub-tasks, exactly where the aggregate SE rises. On the harness's own illustrative base the floor-0.90 selection is 5 sub-tasks with true SE 0.0171; admitting all 7 gives 0.0220 -- 1.29x worse, not 1.53x better.

The named FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX hazard is confirmed and has a worse second face (CC-4): 0.0722 goes from 1.00 SE to 1.53 SE under the widening, and under the halving the prereg's load-bearing disclosure inverts -- 0.0722 is 1.58x the span-scale effect COUPLED would now assert, so a clean-base format channel could exceed the entire effect. The receipt keeps printing the pre-delta ratio because line 1062 hardcodes /0.0909. FI_DOSE_FRACTION (CC-5) is conservative in direction as the brief suspected, but its bar falls 2x while its noise falls 1.53x, and the FI slope turns out to be a two-point fit of a folded statistic with a positive null mean -- false downgrades rise from ~0.001-0.016 to ~0.038-0.108, eating the sensitivity the delta was bought for, and the sole --dry fixture cannot detect the bar change.

What SURVIVES, stated plainly so this is not a manufactured pile-on: FALSE_RECOVERY_ANALYTIC_MAX is genuinely INVARIANT -- I recomputed the exhaustive q-sweep and reproduced 0.1600 / 0.0787 / 0.0663 exactly; the maximum is a function of q = P(s >= bar) and n only, with no dependence on the bar's value or the item count, and the R5.6 FPR of 0.041 stays under the n=5 cap. ESTIMATOR_RECOVERY_FLOOR (a quantile selector), AUROC_FLOOR (rank-based, and it gets EASIER as the SE falls), MAX_FIRE and the conformal rank k = floor(0.15*25) = 3 (depends on n_derive_nulls, not items), DESTRUCTION_KNOWLEDGE_MAX (a different channel entirely), MIN_ESTIMATOR_POOL, MIN_PAIRS_PER_SEED, MIN_ADMISSIBLE_SEEDS and MIN_GATE_SEEDS_REPLICATING are all invariant or strictly improving under both deltas. ZERO_SLOPE_ATOM_MAX strengthens: the slope lattice goes from 1/192 to 1/448, making a zero atom rarer. ESTIMATOR_MDE80 stays interpretable in absolute and span units -- its problem is reachability (CC-1), not scale. The 4dp rounding in paired_delta and _estimator_aggregate remains negligible at 112 items (0.3% of the halved bar).

The two deltas are not separable on this lens. Widening ALONE, correctly implemented and correctly costed, is defensible and actually improves the estimator gate (MDE80 8.0 -> 4.0). It is the bar halving that breaks CC-1, CC-4 and CC-5, all through constants that were defined against MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE. The redesign is: denominate INJECTION_GRID in absolute slope units or in multiples of the price-of-interest slope; pin the FI downgrade bar at its absolute value; derive FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX from the realized selection at --calibrate; fix the variance model; and state honestly what the realized item count will be rather than assuming 112.

Halving the bar is not corruptly tuned -- I checked, and on the only realized data (cycle-43) the halved bar does NOT manufacture COUPLED; it turns BOUNDED_NULL into PARTIAL. But it is unearned and unnecessary, and the delta as written is unsafe on three independent counts. (1) The power model that motivates the halving contradicts the structural zero the SAME panel confirmed 5/5. estimator_power_prefreeze.py models the structural zero in the NOISE (slope_se = sigma_d/4, :44-53) but not in the SIGNAL: it calls rates(MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE, se), i.e. sets the true effect equal to the bar. Since slope == d8/4 and the one measured price is a level drop of 0.0909 AT rank 8 (RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL_2026_07_14.md:26), a seed paying that price has true slope 0.0227 = 1.495x the bar, not 1.00x. The symmetry ceiling -- the whole stated rationale -- does not bind. Recomputed at the anchor's own 112-item SE: bar UNCHANGED gives sensitivity 0.920 at FPR 0.002; the proposal gives 0.991 at FPR 0.042. Keeping the bar beats the proposal's own advertised point (0.880/0.041) on BOTH legs. The goalpost move buys nothing. (2) Change #1 is already the code's behaviour -- select_disjoint (capability_battery_gen.py:369-374) returns EVERY sub-task above the floor; MIN_DISJOINT only sets an ok flag. So "jointly necessary and individually insufficient" pairs a no-op with a real threshold move. (3) The bar is the unit of the estimator gate (INJECTION_GRID in multiples of MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE), so halving it doubles pool noise in gate units and drives MDE80 off the entire grid on the projected realized pool -> VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive, which R5.3 makes TERMINAL. The delta would likely end attempt 4 before any claim-bearing branch. Recommended: drop change #2, restate change #1 as the disclosure it actually is, and fix R5.4/R5.5/R5.6's signal mapping. That version is strictly better on provenance AND on the power table.

findings.title: The power model justifying the halved bar contradicts the structural zero panel #7 confirmed; correcting it removes the need for the goalpost move entirely
findings.claim: R5.6's entire rationale -- 'the design sets the decision THRESHOLD equal to the EFFECT it wants to detect, which makes a paying seed a coin flip by symmetry' -- is false under the estimator the design actually uses. The bar already sits at two-thirds of the price of interest, and once the signal is mapped correctly, widening the selection alone delivers MORE power at 20x LOWER false-positive rate than the proposal.
findings.evidence: C:/Users/heyzo/clawd/styxx/papers/calib-poison-general/estimator_power_prefreeze.py:44-53 (`slope_se_from_agg_se`) DOES model the structural zero: `return sigma_delta * 2.0 / sxx` with sxx=8, i.e. slope SE = sigma_d/4. But :56-67 `rates(true_slope, se, bar)` is called at :93, :108, :158 as `rates(MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE, se, ...)` -- the true effect is set EQUAL to the bar. The two are inconsistent: the same file's section 3 (:117-134) prints `bar 0.0152 <=> d8 >= 0.0608` while `PRICE_OF_INTEREST = 0.0909` (:31). The 0.0909 is a LEVEL drop measured AT r_star=8 (RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL_2026_07_14.md:26: `| 0 | coupled | 8 | 0.671 | 0.7273 | 0.0909 |`), and _estimator_noise_anchor.json:17-53 confirms d(rank 4)=0 in 5/5 seeds, so slope == d8/4 exactly. A seed reproducing the one measured price therefore has true slope 0.0909/4 = 0.02272 = 1.495x the 0.0152 bar. I re-ran the power script's own `rates()` with that corrected true slope (200k sims, same rule as coupling_confirm_v4.py:480-484): 112 items / bar 0.0152 -> sensitivity 0.920, FPR 0.002. 112 items / bar 0.0076 -> 0.991, FPR 0.042. 48 items / bar 0.0152 -> 0.801, FPR 0.021 -- which also falsifies R5.6's categorical 'At 48 items NO bar placement reaches 0.80'. I reproduced R5.6's published table exactly (112/0.0152 -> 0.499/0.002; 112/0.0076 -> 0.877/0.042; 48/0.0076 -> 0.622/0.064) confirming every published number was generated with true_slope == bar.
findings.fix: Withdraw change #2. Correct estimator_power_prefreeze.py to evaluate power at the structural-zero-consistent true slope (price at the crossing divided by 4, = 0.02272), rerun R5.4/R5.6, and freeze with MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE unchanged at 0.0152. Record in the prereg that 0.0152 corresponds to d8 = 0.0608 = 0.669 of the price of interest, so the bar is already BELOW the price and no further loosening is needed to reach 0.80 power.

findings.title: The estimator gate is denominated in MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE, so halving the bar doubles the pool noise in gate units and drives the TERMINAL estimator VOID
findings.claim: Recovery in the shipped estimator gate depends only on the ratio poolSD/bar. Halving the bar doubles that ratio, pushing ESTIMATOR_MDE80 off the entire injection grid on the realized pool -- returning VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive, which R5.3 declares TERMINAL for attempt 4. The advertised 0.880 sensitivity is conditional on a gate the same delta makes likely to fail first.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:137 `INJECTION_GRID = (...)  # multiples of MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE`; :581 injects `+ k * MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE`; :562-564 `_frozen_rule_rates` decides at `MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE`. Pool slopes come from real data (:520-554) and do NOT scale with the bar, so recovery is a function of poolSD/bar alone. Taking the realized pool from _estimator_noise_anchor.json `realized_pool.slopes` (SD 0.09707 on the T/F channel) and scaling by the anchor's OWN projection factors (`projection.rows`: paired SD 0.06124/0.10138 at 48 items, 0.04009/0.10138 at 112), I ran the gate's own draw rule (20k runs, n_draw=5, n_adm=5): 112 items, bar 0.0152 -> poolSD/bar 2.53, MDE80 = 4.0x (gate PASSES, recovery 1.000 at k=4). 112 items, bar 0.0076 -> poolSD/bar 5.05, recovery tops out at 0.742 at k=8 -> MDE80 NOT REACHED -> VOID. 48 items, bar 0.0152 -> MDE80 = 8.0 (passes at the grid top); 48 items, bar 0.0076 -> 0.548 at k=8, VOID. And that is before `_estimator_mde80` takes the MAX over 5 leave-one-seed-out replicates (:663-671), which can only make it worse. Separately, the grid-top's documented justification at :139-144 ('8x bar = 0.1216, a span-scale effect of 0.7296 ... consuming 73% of the aggregate's full range') becomes 0.0608 / 0.3648 = 36% under the halved bar -- the stated basis for the insensitivity threshold is no longer true.
findings.fix: If any bar change is ever contemplated, INJECTION_GRID and its span-scale justification must be re-derived in absolute slope units, and the estimator gate's pass probability must be simulated on the realized pool BEFORE the freeze and published beside the sensitivity table. With change #2 withdrawn per F1, the gate passes at MDE80 = 4x bar on the projected 112-item pool and no re-derivation is needed.

findings.title: Change #1 is already the implemented behaviour, so the 'jointly necessary' pairing that legitimises the bar move is not real
findings.claim: R5.6 packages a no-op with a genuine threshold move and presents them as inseparable. The selection code already keeps every qualifying sub-task; MIN_DISJOINT has never been a target. The realized item count is a data-dependent outcome of a --calibrate that has not been run, so the projected SE 0.0153 -> 0.0100 is not something the delta delivers.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_gen.py:369-374 `select_disjoint`: `survivors = sorted([n for n in GEN_DISJOINT_POOL if clean_scores.get(n, 0.0) >= floor]); return survivors, bool(len(survivors) >= need)` -- every sub-task above the 0.90 floor is kept, and `need` only sets the ok flag. coupling_confirm_v4.py:1042 calls it with `need=MIN_DISJOINT`; :220 uses `len(selected or []) >= MIN_DISJOINT` as a preflight floor; :912 aggregates over all `selected`. There is no truncation to 3 anywhere in the harness. R5.6 item 1's premise -- '`MIN_DISJOINT = 3` (48 items) is a floor being used as a target' -- is false about the code. How many of the 7 sub-tasks clear 0.90 on Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct is unknown until run_calibrate() executes; if 4 clear it the item count is 64 and the projected SE is 0.0132, not 0.0100.
findings.fix: Restate item 1 as a DISCLOSURE, not a change: the selection already keeps all qualifying sub-tasks, and the prereg's '48 items' figures are a worst-case floor. Run --calibrate first and freeze the power arithmetic against the REALIZED item count. Then judge change #2 on its own, unpaired, merits -- which per F1 do not survive.

findings.title: What COUPLED asserts after the change is misstated by a factor of two, and the verdict string does not carry the bar
findings.claim: R5.6 concedes COUPLED would assert 'a price at or above HALF the minimum price of interest'. In the estimator's own units it is ONE THIRD. The concession is computed in span-6 units while the estimator lives in span-4 units -- the same unit confusion as F1, propagated into the honesty disclosure. Meanwhile the verdict string is unchanged, so a reader of the receipt cannot tell a full-price COUPLED from a third-price one.
findings.evidence: estimator_power_prefreeze.py:120 `d8_needed = MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE * 4.0`. At bar 0.0152, d8 = 0.0608 = 0.669 of the 0.0909 price. At bar 0.0076, d8 = 0.0304 = 0.334 of it. So the delta moves COUPLED's assertion from 'at least two-thirds of the only measured price at the crossing' to 'at least one third'. coupling_confirm_v4.py:196 `COUPLED__dose_slope_price_measured_1p5B` is unchanged by the delta and carries no bar token -- whereas panel #7 R3 specifically renamed the NEGATIVE branch to `NO_PAIRED_PRICE_ABOVE_ESTIMATOR_MDE80__battery_not_deaf_1p5B` (:201, prereg :141-148) precisely so the negative claim would name its own resolution. Asymmetric: the silence-side claim is forced to disclose its weakness on the string; the paper-restarting claim is weakened silently.
findings.fix: If any bar change survives, the COUPLED string must carry its own resolution the way the bounded null now does (e.g. a token naming the d8-equivalent as a fraction of the price of interest), and the concession sentence must be restated in estimator units (one third, not half). With change #2 withdrawn this finding closes.

findings.title: FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX = 0.0722 silently loosens under the wider selection -- the hazard is real and confirmed by arithmetic
findings.claim: The calibrate-time refuse-to-train gate is documented and derived as exactly ONE per-checkpoint SE at 48 items. At 112 items the same literal becomes 1.53 SE (worst case) or 2.55 SE at the battery's own 0.90 selection floor, so the format-invariance guard admits a materially larger format channel with no one having decided that.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:123 `FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX = 0.0722   # PREREG: one per-checkpoint aggregate SE at minimum selection`; PREREG:249-251 derives it as sqrt(0.25/48) = 0.0722; enforced at :1048-1049 (`fi_ok`) and folded into `sel['ok']` at :1050, which run_real ENFORCES via preflight (:217). Computed SEs: n=48 p=0.5 -> 0.0722 (1.00 SE, as documented); n=112 p=0.5 -> 0.0472 (1.53 SE); n=112 p=0.9 -> 0.0283 (2.55 SE). Compounding with change #2: PREREG:252 states the bar as '0.7938 of the 0.0909 minimum price of interest'; under the halved bar the same 0.0722 is 2.4x the d8 the COUPLED bar requires (0.0304). A format channel more than twice the size of the effect being priced would pass the clean-base gate. The companion receipt field `format_invariance_max_fraction_of_price` (:1062) hard-codes the 0.0909 divisor and so keeps reporting a number that no longer describes the guard's stringency.
findings.fix: Re-derive FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX from the REALIZED selection size at --calibrate time (compute it as sqrt(p(1-p)/n_items) on the realized selected aggregate rather than freezing a literal), or freeze it in SE units. Update PREREG:249-254 and the :1062 receipt field to state the realized multiple.

findings.title: The advertised operating point prices only the slope rule; the FI demotion bar halves with the price bar and eats a third of the claimed sensitivity gain
findings.claim: 0.880 is the sensitivity of the per-seed slope rule in isolation. The verdict must additionally survive the estimator gate, the subtractive guard check, and the trained-FI dose demotion -- and that last one is denominated in MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE, so halving the price bar also halves the FI demotion bar to 0.0038, well inside the FI slope's own noise. The realized COUPLED sensitivity is materially below the number used to justify the goalpost move.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:114 `FI_DOSE_FRACTION = 0.5` and :728 `bar = FI_DOSE_FRACTION * MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE` -> 0.0076 today, 0.0038 under the delta. The FI slope is fit at :733-737 over only the in-span checkpoints, which per the audit schedule (:935, :951-953) are the rank-2 pin and the first crossing at rank 8 -- TWO points, so slope = (fi_8 - fi_2)/6. With abs_delta SE = sqrt(2)*se_agg and se_agg = sqrt(0.9*0.1/112) = 0.0283, the FI-slope null SD is 0.00945. P(a seed flagged under zero true drift) = 0.344 at bar 0.0038 vs 0.211 at bar 0.0076; P(strict majority of 5 flagged -> COUPLED auto-demoted to PARTIAL at :831-832) = 0.226 vs 0.066. So the delta triples the spurious-demotion rate and the composite sensitivity is roughly 0.880 x 0.774 = 0.68 before the estimator gate (F2) and the subtractive check are applied at all.
findings.fix: Publish a COMPOSITE operating point -- estimator gate pass x slope rule x subtractive x FI-demotion -- not the slope rule alone, and decide explicitly whether FI_DOSE_FRACTION should track MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE or be frozen in absolute units against the FI channel's own measured noise.

findings.title: The --dry 52/52 is blind to the delta and must not be quoted as evidence for it
findings.claim: The existing regression suite cannot detect any of the hazards above. I ran it at both bars and nothing changed.
findings.evidence: I imported coupling_confirm_v4.py, ran run_dry() (52 checks, all_ok=True), then set `m.MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE = 0.0076` and re-ran: all_ok=True, 52 checks, ZERO checks changed state. The reason is fixture construction -- the common-mode noise `nz()` (:1202-1209) is applied identically to a seed's accumulate and fixed arms so every paired delta cancels, and every fixture slope is exactly 0 (`seed_flat`, :1272-1274) or +0.02 / -0.03 (`seed_coupled` :1268, `seed_partial` :1292-1297). No fixture lands in the (0.0076, 0.0152) band the delta opens. The estimator gate is equally blind on these fixtures: `bounded_null_carries_mde80` reports MDE80 = 0.0228 (1.5x bar) at the current bar and 0.0114 (still 1.5x bar) at the halved bar -- the fixture pool is too quiet for the poolSD/bar doubling to bite, and note that the reported `span_bound` (the level the bounded null claims to be bounded above) silently halves from 0.1368 to 0.0684 on IDENTICAL data.
findings.fix: Add a fixture with per-seed slopes in (0.0076, 0.0152) that must read BOUNDED_NULL under the frozen bar, and a fixture whose estimator pool SD is set from _estimator_noise_anchor.json's realized scale rather than NZ=0.012, so the gate's real-data behaviour is exercised. Do not cite '--dry 52/52' in support of any threshold change.

findings.title: Numeral collision and stale derivation text across prereg and receipts
findings.claim: 0.0076 currently names the FI demotion bar. Making it the price bar makes every existing '0.0076' in the prereg, code comments and receipts ambiguous, and several derivation sentences become false.
findings.evidence: PREREG:171 and :264-266 both state the FI demotion threshold as 'one-half of MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE (0.5 x 0.0152 = 0.0076)'; coupling_confirm_v4.py:1323 carries the same literal in a fixture comment (`FI slope 0.06/6 = 0.01 >= 0.0076`). Under the delta the FI bar becomes 0.0038 and 0.0076 becomes the price bar -- the same numeral naming two different guards across the document set. Stale-on-delta derivation text: PREREG:285 ('0.0909 / (8 - 2) = 0.015150'), PREREG:366-369 (the symmetry claim, false per F1), PREREG:350-351 and :353-354 (the 0.0722 / 0.1021 / 0.036 SE chain), coupling_confirm_v4.py:107 comment, :139-144 (grid-top rationale), :1062 (`format_invariance_max_fraction_of_price`), and estimator_power_prefreeze.py:185-191 (`conclusion`, which states power 'lands near 0.24' while R5.4 in the prereg quotes 0.33 for the same configuration -- an existing inconsistency between the script's own output text and the prereg section it feeds).
findings.fix: Reconcile the R5.4 0.33 / script 0.24 discrepancy, and if any threshold ever moves, sweep every literal 0.0152 / 0.0076 / 0.0722 in the prereg, harness comments and dry fixtures in one commit so no numeral names two guards.

lens_reviews.lens: does-it-answer-the-question (end-to-end inferential chain of the R5.6 v6 delta)

findings.title: Halving MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE flips the estimator gate from PASS to a TERMINAL VOID
findings.claim: The R5.6 power table was computed by estimator_power_prefreeze.rates() (estimator_power_prefreeze.py:56-66), a standalone Gaussian model of the DOSE rule only. It never runs the delta through estimator_admissibility(), which coupling_confirm_v4.py:808-811 places in front of ALL FIVE claim-bearing branches. INJECTION_GRID is in multiples of MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE (:137) and the injection is applied as k*MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE (:581), while pool noise is fixed absolute. Halving the bar therefore halves every absolute injection AND halves the -bar veto threshold: P(detect)=P(+/-s >= (1-k)bar) falls and P(veto)=P(+/-s <= -(1+k)bar) rises. Both legs move against recovery at every grid multiple.
findings.evidence:
Driving the harness's own _estimator_mde80 + the full 5 leave-one-seed-out replicate loop (coupling_confirm_v4.py:660-670) on the measured pool from _estimator_noise_anchor.json scaled to the projected 112-item noise (SE ratio 0.494):
  bar=0.0152 -> reps {full 5.0, loo0 5.0, loo1 5.0, loo2 5.0, loo3 4.0, loo4 3.0} = PASS, MDE80 5x bar
  bar=0.0076 -> reps {full None, loo0 None, loo1 None, loo2 None, loo3 8.0, loo4 6.0} = VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive
At the raw measured (24-item) pool both bars VOID. R5.3 makes that VOID TERMINAL for the prereg. So the delta sold as raising sensitivity 0.498->0.880 in fact raises the probability of the one outcome that permanently ends attempt 4 before any verdict is reachable. Realized P(COUPLED) under the delta is not 0.880; it is approximately 0.
findings.fix: Do not halve MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE. If a lower bar is still wanted after the other findings, INJECTION_GRID must be re-expressed in ABSOLUTE slope units (or in units of the price of interest, which does not move) so the estimator gate's reach is decoupled from the decision bar, and the gate must then be re-derived and re-paneled with that change stated. Either way, any future power table MUST be produced by driving compute_verdict/estimator_admissibility end-to-end on simulated curves, never by a standalone rates() model of one branch.

findings.title: The prereg's decision basis is refuted by the program's own corrected anchor script; the receipt on disk was stale
findings.claim: PREREG R5.6 (lines 629-665) and R5.4 (589-593) quote slope SE 0.0100 at 112 items, sensitivity 0.880 at the halved bar, and 'At 48 items NO bar placement reaches 0.80, so widening the selection is not optional.' estimator_noise_anchor.py as it currently stands on disk retracts all three: its section 9(d) is literally headed 'CORRECTION ... SUBTRACTS a claim this same session made three hours ago'. The .py had been corrected but NOT re-run, so _estimator_noise_anchor.json still carried the superseded assumed_p=0.9 rows that R5.6 cites.
findings.evidence: Running estimator_noise_anchor.py (zero GPU, deterministic, gitignored artifact) regenerates the receipt: clean base accuracy 1.0 but REALIZED fine-tuned fixed-arm accuracy 0.8625 is the operative value, plus a not-well-identified trajectory floor of 0.0198 that does NOT shrink with items. Corrected rows: 48 items slope SE 0.01826 (1.20x bar), 112 items 0.01253 (0.82x bar, NOT 0.66x), 224 items 0.00953, infinite items 0.00496. Corrected operating points: 112 items + bar 0.0076 -> sensitivity 0.7548, clears_080 FALSE (not 0.880); 224 items + bar 0.0076 -> 0.8992. The script's own conclusion: 'Reaching about 0.90 needs roughly 224 items ... the battery must be EXPANDED, not merely fully selected.' Every operating point in the R5.6 table is stale.
findings.fix: Delete the R5.6 table and rewrite the section against the regenerated receipt. State the trajectory floor (0.0198, unidentified, band 0 to 0.03) explicitly, because at the halved bar of 0.0076 the pessimistic floor's slope-SE contribution (0.0075) equals the bar and no number of items helps. Re-run BOTH zero-GPU scripts and commit their receipts in the same change as the prereg text, so a reader can never be reading a decision against a receipt that does not reproduce.

findings.title: Delta #1 is a no-op: the harness already selects every qualifying sub-task and already decodes all of them
findings.claim: R5.6 asserts 'MIN_DISJOINT = 3 (48 items) is a floor being used as a target' and 'FREE PRECISION IS AVAILABLE AND UNUSED ... Cost is decode time only.' Both are misreadings of the code. There is nothing to change and no cost to pay; the item count is set entirely by DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN=0.90 against the clean base's per-sub-task gen accuracy, which has never been measured.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_gen.py:369-374 select_disjoint returns `survivors = sorted([n for n in GEN_DISJOINT_POOL if clean_scores.get(n,0.0) >= floor])` -- ALL qualifiers, never truncated to `need`. `need` only sets the ok flag. coupling_confirm_v4.py:1042 passes need=MIN_DISJOINT; MIN_DISJOINT is used nowhere else except the preflight count guard at :220 and the receipts at :842/:1052. Separately, capability_battery_gen.py:337 measure_all_gen loops `GEN_DISJOINT_POOL.items() + GEN_BANK_ADJACENT.items()` unconditionally and coupling_confirm_v4.py:910 calls it with no subset, so all 7 disjoint sub-tasks plus MUL_GEN are ALREADY decoded at every checkpoint of every arm. Selection only chooses which pre-computed accuracies enter the aggregate. So the only way to reach 112 items is for the base to clear 0.90 on all 7 -- in which case it happens today with no delta -- or to LOWER DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN, which is a threshold loosening with entirely different provenance, and which admits sub-tasks at p<0.90 whose higher per-item variance partly cancels the sqrt(n) gain.
findings.fix: Strike delta #1. Replace it with the only real lever: run `--calibrate` BEFORE the freeze (it is base-only and treatment-blind, so it cannot leak arm outcomes) to measure how many of the 7 gen sub-tasks clear 0.90, and write the realized item count into the prereg. Freeze the bar rule as a fraction of the price of interest BEFORE reading that count, and say so in the document. If the count is short of what the corrected anchor requires (~224 items), the honest delta is to EXPAND the battery (7 x 32) with a fresh selftest, not to move any bar.

findings.title: FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX silently loosens under both deltas -- an existing guard breaks with no code change
findings.claim: FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX = 0.0722 (coupling_confirm_v4.py:123) is documented as 'one per-checkpoint aggregate SE at minimum selection' and is enforced as a fixed absolute number at :1048-1049 against the SELECTED-aggregate abs_delta. Its derivation is false under either delta, and the constant does not track. The prereg already concedes at lines 252-254 that 0.0722 = 0.79 of the price of interest means 'a format channel nearly four-fifths the size of the effect being priced can pass the clean-base gate' -- and calls that uncomfortable. Under the halved bar that sentence becomes indefensible.
findings.evidence: Arithmetic. In SE units: at 48 items sqrt(0.25/48)=0.0722, so the bar is exactly 1.00 worst-case SE (1.67 SE at p=0.9). At 112 items sqrt(0.25/112)=0.0472, so the same 0.0722 is 1.53 worst-case SE and 2.55 SE at p=0.9 -- the guard loosens by 1.53x with zero code change. Against the effect: the smallest COUPLED-qualifying span-scale effect is bar x 6. At bar 0.0152 that is 0.0912 and 0.0722 is 0.79x it (as the prereg states). At bar 0.0076 it is 0.0456 and 0.0722 is 1.58x it -- a clean-base format channel LARGER than the entire smallest effect the design would certify as COUPLED passes the gate.
findings.fix: Make FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX derived rather than literal: compute it at calibrate time as sqrt(0.25/(16*len(selected))) so it tracks the realized selection, AND add a second, binding leg tying it to the decision bar (e.g. abs_delta <= 0.25 * MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE * span) so the format allowance can never exceed a fixed small fraction of the smallest certifiable effect. Both are outcome-blind and derivable today. Also fix the hard-coded 0.0909 literal in the receipt field `format_invariance_max_fraction_of_price` at :1062, which will report against the wrong denominator under the delta.

findings.title: Halving the bar halves the FI demotion bar, adding large unmodelled COUPLED loss the 0.880 headline ignores
findings.claim: trained_fi_dose_graded computes `bar = FI_DOSE_FRACTION * MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE` (coupling_confirm_v4.py:728), so halving MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE halves the demotion bar from 0.0076 to 0.0038 slope, i.e. the required FI abs_delta rise across the span drops from 0.0456 to 0.0228. The demotion direction is conservative (COUPLED -> PARTIAL), so this is not an unearned-verdict fatal, but it is a materially larger false-demotion rate that the R5.6 sensitivity figure does not include: the realized P(COUPLED) is sensitivity x P(survive subtractive) x P(survive FI demotion), and only the first factor is quoted.
findings.evidence: The FI fit uses accumulate-arm points with format_invariance in [2,8] (:733-737). Those exist only at the rank-2 pin (audit at :935, step 0) and the first crossing checkpoint (:951-953); the final audit (:982) is at rank ~24, out of span. So the FI slope is a TWO-point statistic, (fi8 - fi2)/6 -- and fi2 is measured on the base model with a zero-initialized LoRA, identical across all seeds and arms, so it carries no seed-level variance at all. It is a second one-free-point statistic, structurally parallel to the d8/4 problem. Taking fi8's sampling SD as sqrt(discordance/n): at 112 items and 10% wrapper discordance, SD 0.0299, P(flag) rises from 0.064 (threshold 0.0456) to 0.223 (threshold 0.0228), and P(strict majority of 5 flagged | true COUPLED) rises from 0.002 to 0.077. At 20% discordance, 0.077 -> 0.223 with majority 0.007 -> 0.156. A 10-35x relative increase in false demotion, entirely absent from the table.
findings.fix: Decouple FI_DOSE_FRACTION from MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE: express the FI demotion bar in absolute aggregate units derived from the price of interest (which does not move), not as a fraction of a decision threshold that the delta is changing. Separately, disclose that the FI slope is a two-point statistic anchored on a zero-variance base measurement, and report the realized fi2/fi8 pair, not just the slope.

findings.title: The bounded null -- the ANTICIPATED verdict -- gains nothing from the delta; MDE80 in absolute terms is a pool property
findings.claim: The prereg's own ANTICIPATED VERDICT section (lines 424-432) says the expected outcome is the bounded null or PARTIAL. The bounded null's shipped claim level is estimator_mde80 = mult * MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE (coupling_confirm_v4.py:673) with span_bound = mde80 * 6 (:675). Halving the bar halves the unit but raises the multiple by exactly the compensating factor, because the absolute detectable effect is set by the pool noise, not by the bar. The delta therefore buys zero improvement on the branch the design expects to land on, while costing the estimator gate (F1).
findings.evidence: At the projected 224-item pool the harness returns MDE80 multiple 4.0 at bar 0.0152 and 8.0 at bar 0.0076. Absolute: 4 x 0.0152 = 0.0608 and 8 x 0.0076 = 0.0608 -- identical to four decimal places. Span-scale bound 0.365 in both cases, versus a price of interest of 0.0909. So under either bar the bounded null licenses only 'no paired price above ~0.365 over the span', roughly 4x weaker than the question the paper asks, and the delta does not move that number at all.
findings.fix: State this explicitly in R5.6 so the trade is legible: the delta is a bet purely on the COUPLED branch, which the prereg itself calls the SURPRISE, purchased at the cost of the terminal-VOID risk in F1 and paid on a branch expected not to occur. If the goal is a publishable negative, the lever is items (F3/F9), not the bar.

findings.title: A known-3.8x-inflated null holds an unappealable kill switch (lens b: conservatism vs terminal VOID)
findings.claim: R5.3 makes VOID_COUPLING__estimator_* TERMINAL, justified as anti-forking-paths. That clause was written before the pool's conservatism was measured. The two facts are now jointly incoherent: the program knows its gate's null is a between-seed contrast standing in for a within-seed one and overstates the real estimator noise by a MEASURED factor, and it simultaneously makes that gate's failure unappealable and fatal to the attempt. That is a false-VOID risk with no disclosure path and no remedy.
findings.evidence: _estimator_noise_anchor.json within_seed_paired_noise: implied_slope_se 0.02534 within-seed vs pool_slope_sd 0.09707, pool_overstatement_factor 3.83, with the note that rank 4 is excluded because it is the structural zero proving the arms share history. Panel #7's own residual R-2 already named this ('The pool is a between-seed contrast standing in for a within-seed cross-arm contrast'). Empirically the inflation is decisive, not marginal: on the raw measured pool the gate VOIDs at BOTH bars (F1 evidence), including at the current frozen bar. Given R5.3, a run that is statistically fine on its real within-seed noise can be killed permanently by a null the program has already documented as 3.8x too wide.
findings.fix: Fix the pool BEFORE the freeze, outcome-blind: build the estimator null as a WITHIN-seed contrast on the same code path -- e.g. per seed, regress the fixed arm's own aggregate on the accumulate arm's erased_rank over [2,8] (5 units, same _per_seed_slope, same 4dp lattice, no between-seed trajectory term) -- and report the cross-seed pool beside it as a disclosed conservative second tier. MIN_ESTIMATOR_POOL would need to drop to 5 with that stated. If the pool construction is left as-is, R5.3's terminality must be softened to: VOID is terminal for the COUPLED branch only, and the run may still ship a bounded null carrying the pool-based (conservative) MDE80 with the 3.8x overstatement printed on the certificate.

findings.title: 'Dose-response' names a two-point contrast; the rank-6 checkpoint cannot move any verdict (lens c)
findings.claim: With fitted ranks {4,6,8}, d4 is a structural zero (arms bit-identical through step 24; CONFIRMED 5/5 in the anchor) and rank 6 sits exactly at the fitted centroid, so its OLS leverage is exactly zero. The per-seed slope is algebraically d8/4. The dose axis contributes ONE free point, and no verdict, receipt or claim can depend on the rank-6 measurement. Calling the finding a dose-RESPONSE, and gating it on an OLS slope, dresses a single paired contrast at the crossing rank as a regression.
findings.evidence: slope = [(4-6)*d4 + (6-6)*d6 + (8-6)*d8] / 8 = (0 + 0 + 2*d8)/8 = d8/4 exactly. Confirmed structurally in v4: coupling_confirm_v4.py:937-953 rebinds U only inside the refit branch, so at the step-25 audit both arms have taken 25 identical optimizer steps. Anchor structural_zero_test: d_step25 = 0.0 in 5 of 5 seeds. Changing the refit cadence does NOT help -- rank grows ~2 per refit, so a faster cadence puts FEWER checkpoints inside [2,8] and a slower one yields the same {4,6,8} set at later steps. The span cannot widen (ranks 10-24 are read-recovery). The geometry is fixed.
findings.fix: Rename the statistic and the verdict string to what they are: the paired capability delta at the crossing rank (scaled by 1/4), not a dose slope. Drop 'dose_slope' from COUPLED__dose_slope_price_measured_1p5B and drop the OLS wrapper in favour of reporting d8 directly with its binomial interval -- which also makes the whole power analysis a one-sample binomial problem instead of a regression fiction. This is free, it subtracts a claim the design cannot support, and per the house rule that is exactly the kind of post-hoc analysis that is licensed.

findings.title: Item-level pairing is discarded before differencing -- 1.7-2.5x free precision the arc has never considered (lens e)
findings.claim: The two arms answer the SAME items at the SAME step with the same decoder, but the pipeline aggregates to a scalar accuracy per sub-task and throws the per-item correctness vector away, so the paired delta is computed as a difference of two effectively independent binomials (sqrt(2) x SE). The correct paired statistic is McNemar on the discordant items, SE = sqrt(discordance/n). Two near-identical fine-tuned checkpoints typically disagree on well under 10% of items, so the discarded pairing is worth more precision than the entire proposed selection widening -- at ZERO additional GPU.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_gen.py:303-323 measure_gen_subtask returns only {'acc','acc_guard_excl','repetition_fires','echo_fires','n','n_scored','correct'} -- the per-item score vector is accumulated into `correct` and dropped. capability_battery_gen.py:389-398 paired_delta then differences two rounded aggregates. Arithmetic at 112 items: independent-arms SE(delta) = sqrt(2*0.09/112) = 0.0401; paired SE at 6% discordance = sqrt(0.06/112) = 0.0231 (1.73x tighter, slope SE 0.0058); at 3% discordance = 0.0164 (2.45x tighter, slope SE 0.0041). The 2.45x case alone beats the 224-item expansion the corrected anchor says is otherwise required (slope SE 0.0095), with no extra decode at all.
findings.fix: Add a per-item correctness bitvector to the checkpoint record (a schema addition, no extra GPU) and recompute the paired delta as the McNemar discordance statistic. Measure the actual arm-to-arm discordance at --smoke before choosing any bar, because it sets the noise the bar is being placed against and it is currently unmeasured. Bonus: the sign test over discordant items gives a legitimate per-seed p at n~7-15 pairs, partly recovering the significance the design surrendered at 5 seeds.

findings.title: The power table ignores the aggregate lattice; the realized halved bar is 17.5% above nominal
findings.claim: estimator_power_prefreeze.rates() (:56-66) draws continuous Gaussians, but the per-seed statistic is d8/4 with d8 on a 1/n_items lattice, so the effective slope lattice is 1/(4*n_items) and every nominal bar snaps UP to the next lattice point. The table's sensitivities are therefore slightly optimistic, and the 'plateau' provenance defence is really a lattice tread.
findings.evidence: At 112 items the slope lattice is 0.00223. Nominal bar 0.0076 realizes as 4/112 -> 0.00893 (+17.5%); nominal 0.0152 realizes as 0.01562 (+2.8%). Simulating at slope SE 0.0100 (the stale figure R5.6 used): sensitivity 0.879 -> 0.853 and FPR 0.041 -> 0.030. Bar fractions 0.6 and 0.5 both snap to 4/112 and 0.4 and 0.33 both snap to 3/112, so the claimed 0.845-0.886 'plateau across bar fractions 0.6 to 0.33' is two lattice treads, not a smooth plateau -- the not-knife-edge conclusion survives but for a different reason than stated.
findings.fix: Quantize the bar to the realized lattice in any future power computation and quote the realized bar (e.g. 4/112 = 0.00893, i.e. 'four more of 112 items wrong at step 75') rather than the nominal one, since that is the sentence a reader can actually check against the points surface.

findings.title: Receipt and prose fields hard-code 48-item / whole-price arithmetic and go stale under either delta
findings.claim: Several receipt fields and prereg sentences encode the current configuration as literals and will silently misreport under the delta.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:1062 hard-codes the price literal: `format_invariance_max_fraction_of_price: round(FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX / 0.0909, 4)`. _thresholds() (:840-858) reports min_disjoint and format_invariance_max but never the realized item count (16 * len(selected)), which is the quantity every noise claim depends on. PREREG lines 350-355 state 'Per-checkpoint aggregate SE at minimum selection (3 sub-tasks x 16 = 48 items) ... at most 0.0722' and derive the 0.036 slope-SE bound from it; lines 250-253 state the 0.7938-of-price framing. All become false under widened selection or a halved bar.
findings.fix: Add `n_items_selected` and `slope_lattice` to _thresholds() and to the result receipt, replace the 0.0909 literal with a named PRICE_OF_INTEREST constant, and regenerate the prereg's SE prose from the realized selection rather than from the MIN_DISJOINT floor.

lens_reviews.summary:
NO_GO_redesign. The delta is not merely mis-tuned; its two headline claims are each refuted by code or by the program's own receipts, and the decisive failure is one the R5.6 arithmetic structurally could not see.

The fatal is F1. INJECTION_GRID is denominated in multiples of MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE, so halving the bar halves every absolute injection while pool noise stays fixed, and it simultaneously loosens the -bar veto. Driving the harness's own _estimator_mde80 plus the full 5-LOO loop on the measured pool scaled to the projected 112-item noise: bar 0.0152 PASSES with MDE80 5x; bar 0.0076 returns None on the full pool and on 4 of 5 LOO replicates, i.e. VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive, which R5.3 makes TERMINAL. The delta advertised as raising sensitivity 0.498 -> 0.880 in fact drives realized P(COUPLED) toward zero by killing the attempt at a gate standing in front of all five claim-bearing branches. The R5.6 table could not detect this because it was produced by estimator_power_prefreeze.rates(), a standalone Gaussian model of the dose rule alone that never touches estimator_admissibility().

F3: delta #1 is a no-op. capability_battery_gen.py:369-374 already returns every sub-task clearing the floor -- MIN_DISJOINT is only an ok-flag -- and capability_battery_gen.py:337 already decodes all 7 disjoint sub-tasks plus MUL_GEN at every checkpoint. There is nothing to select and no decode cost to pay; the item count is set entirely by DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN=0.90 against gen-battery base accuracies that have never been measured.

F2: I re-ran estimator_noise_anchor.py (zero GPU, deterministic, gitignored). The script on disk had been corrected but not re-executed, so _estimator_noise_anchor.json was stale and R5.6 cites the superseded rows. The corrected receipt -- now regenerated at C:/Users/heyzo/clawd/styxx/papers/calib-poison-general/_estimator_noise_anchor.json -- retracts them in its own section 9(d): the 0.90 floor applies to the CLEAN BASE (which scores 1.0), fine-tuning degrades the arms to 0.8625, a not-well-identified trajectory floor of 0.0198 survives on top, and 112 items + halved bar gives sensitivity 0.755, NOT 0.880 and short of the 0.80 convention. Its own conclusion is that ~224 items are needed and the battery must be EXPANDED, not merely fully selected.

F4 confirms the brief's suspected coupled constant and its siblings. FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX=0.0722 is a literal whose derivation ('one SE at minimum selection') is false under either delta: at 112 items it becomes 1.53 worst-case SE (2.55 SE at p=0.9), and against the smallest COUPLED-qualifying span effect it goes from 0.79x to 1.58x -- a clean-base format channel larger than the entire smallest certifiable effect would pass. FI_DOSE_FRACTION x MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE (F5) halves the demotion bar, raising false demotion of a true COUPLED from ~0.002 to ~0.08-0.16, unmodelled in the headline; and I note the FI slope is itself a two-point statistic anchored on a zero-variance base measurement. Two constants survive the delta cleanly and I say so plainly: FALSE_RECOVERY_ANALYTIC_MAX is a maximum over q and is bar-independent, and the split-conformal fire threshold is rank-based and therefore scale-free.

On the mandate lens: (a) the modal reachable verdict under the delta is the terminal estimator VOID; COUPLED at the halved bar would assert a price >= 0.0456 span-scale, exactly 50.2% of the only price ever measured, i.e. inside the region the prereg's own provenance calls 'below this program's resolution of interest' -- it supports 'a dose-graded price at least half the minimum price of interest', not the erasure-bound headline. (b) Yes, there is a real tension and it is now measured: the pool overstates within-seed noise by 3.83x (anchor pool_overstatement_factor) and at that inflation VOIDs even at the CURRENT bar, while R5.3 makes the VOID unappealable -- fix the pool to a within-seed contrast pre-freeze, or soften terminality (F7). (c) No, a dose-RESPONSE claim is not honest: slope = d8/4 exactly, rank 6 has zero leverage, and no cadence or span change can fix it -- rename the statistic (F8). (d) Cheapest decisive experiment: run --calibrate BEFORE the freeze. It is base-only and treatment-blind, costs one bf16 load plus ~240 greedy 8-token decodes (single-digit minutes, no training), and it resolves the single input every R5.6 number is conditional on. Freeze the bar rule as a fraction of the price of interest before reading the count and say so. Zero-GPU runner-up: drive the power model through compute_verdict end-to-end -- one script, and it would have caught F1. (e) Yes: item-level pairing. The arms answer the same items and measure_gen_subtask discards the per-item vector, so paired_delta differences two effectively independent binomials. McNemar on discordant items is 1.73x tighter at 6% discordance and 2.45x at 3% -- the latter beats the 224-item expansion outright, at zero GPU, from a schema change.

The delta's INTENT on precision is right and the corrected anchor endorses it. The execution is wrong in both halves: widening is already done and is not the binding constraint, and halving the bar is actively fatal.

findings.title: Halving MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE flips the estimator gate from PASS to the TERMINAL void, via INJECTION_GRID
findings.claim: INJECTION_GRID is expressed in multiples of MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE and capped at 8x (coupling_confirm_v4.py:137-144). The pool's dispersion is an absolute property of the data, not of the bar. Halving the bar halves the largest injectable ABSOLUTE effect (0.1216 -> 0.0608) while leaving the pool unchanged, so ESTIMATOR_MDE80 falls off the top of the grid and estimator_admissibility returns VOID_COUPLING__estimator_insensitive (coupling_confirm_v4.py:668-670) -- which PREREG R5.3 declares TERMINAL for attempt 4. The delta does not mention INJECTION_GRID at all.
findings.evidence: Ran the shipped draw rule (_pseudo_rates/_estimator_mde80, n_draw=5, n_adm=5) on the delta's OWN realized pool (_estimator_noise_anchor.json realized_pool.slopes, SD 0.09707), rescaled by the delta's OWN noise model. 112 items (x sqrt(24/112)), bar 0.0152: MDE80 = 5.0x -> PASS. Same pool, bar 0.0076: recovery at the grid top (8x) only 0.745, MDE80 = None -> VOID. VOIDs at 0.0076 under all four scalings tested: 48-item item model (PASS 8.0 / VOID), 112-item item model (PASS 5.0 / VOID), 7-sub-task model with no within-sub-task credit (PASS 8.0 / VOID), 5-survivor 80-item (PASS 6.0 / VOID). The shipped gate takes the MAX over full pool + 5 LOO replicates (coupling_confirm_v4.py:660-671), so it is at least as likely to void as my sim. The grid-top comment's own justification also evaporates: '8x bar = 0.1216, a span-scale effect of 0.7296 ... 73% of the aggregate's full range' becomes 0.0608 / 0.3648 / 36.5%.
findings.fix: Re-derive INJECTION_GRID in ABSOLUTE slope units, or in units of PRICE_OF_INTEREST/(hi-lo) = 0.015150 (invariant to the decision bar), BEFORE halving MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE. Then re-run the projection and show the estimator gate still passes. Add a --dry assertion that the grid top, expressed absolutely, is unchanged by any bar change.

findings.title: Delta #1 is a no-op in the code; 112 items is not a design choice but an unmeasured calibration outcome
findings.claim: R5.6 delta #1 ('Select EVERY qualifying disjoint sub-task, not the minimum 3'; R5.4 'MIN_DISJOINT = 3 is a floor being used as a target') describes behaviour the harness ALREADY has. Freezing the pair therefore executes only delta #2 (the halved bar) at an item count nobody controls.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_gen.py:369-374 select_disjoint returns `sorted([n for n in GEN_DISJOINT_POOL if clean_scores.get(n,0.0) >= floor])` -- every survivor; `need` only sets the boolean ok. It is called that way at coupling_confirm_v4.py:1042 (run_calibrate) and :1085. The 3-sub-task/48-item figure is not a target: it is the MEASURED survivor count on the T/F channel, where only 3 of 8 candidates cleared 0.90 (coupling_confirm_disjoint_selected.json base_scores: ORTH_LAST 0.75, ORTH_CONTAINS 0.75, CASE 0.875, ALPHA_AFTER 0.875, SEQ 0.5 all failed). The gen survivor count has never been measured -- SELECTION_CACHE `coupling_v4_gen_selected.json` does not exist on disk; --calibrate has not run. If calibration returns 3 (the only precedent), the frozen design is the 48-item/0.0076 row: sensitivity 0.623 (below the 0.80 the delta exists to reach), FPR 0.063 vs 0.021 as-designed, bounded-null-at-null collapsing 0.808 -> 0.285. Survivor sweep at 16 items/sub-task, bar 0.0076: k=3 sens 0.623, k=4 0.720, k=5 0.789, k=6 0.841, k=7 0.878 -- the delta's headline needs all 7.
findings.fix: Run --calibrate FIRST (cheap GPU, already step 1 of the post-panel sequence), then set the bar as a pre-committed function of the realized survivor count k, or add a preflight VOID when k is below the count the power arithmetic assumed. Delete the claim that selecting all qualifying sub-tasks is a change.

findings.title: The 'no trajectory floor' inference is untestable by the test that was run, and the p-inversion is the same equation
findings.claim: The delta's load-bearing claim -- noise is sampling-dominated, so more items fixes it -- rests on a residual that was arithmetically forced to zero, plus a second 'confirmation' that is the identical equation solved for p.
findings.evidence: estimator_noise_anchor.py:227-238 subtracts samp = sqrt(0.25/24)*sqrt(2) = 0.14434, the p=0.5 worst case. Any measured paired SD below 0.1443 returns resid = 0 by construction; the measured 0.10138 is 0.70x that, so the test could not have detected a floor. Line 252-258 then solves p(1-p) = sd^2*24/2 for the p that makes the residual exactly zero (0.8559) and reports 'no irreducible trajectory floor is detectable' -- the same statement, presented as corroboration. How big a floor can hide: at p=0.90 residual SD 0.0527 -> slope-SE floor 0.0132 (0.87x bar); at p=0.95, 0.0795 -> 0.0199 (1.31x bar). Both exceed the 0.0100 the delta is buying, and no item count moves them. DEFENCE OWED: I measured the realized T/F aggregate over ranks 6,8 both arms from the same file = 0.8042; at p=0.804 the independent-arms sampling SD is 0.1146 > the measured 0.1014, so at the point estimate the sampling model survives on the REALIZED p, not just the worst case. But the two arms score the SAME fixed items and are bit-identical through step 24 (confirmed 5/5), so they are positively correlated; with arm-correlation rho the sampling term is 2p(1-p)(1-rho)/n and the data constrain only the product. rho=0.217 with zero floor, rho=0.50 with a floor of slope SE 0.0152 (1.00x bar), and rho=0.80 with 1.4x bar all fit the SAME measurement exactly. PREREG R5.5's escalation ('which was an assumption in R5.4 and is now evidence') is not earned.
findings.fix: State it as 'no floor DETECTED under a bound that could not have detected one below 1.42x the point estimate'. Price the arm-correlation confound explicitly. Pre-commit to measuring the floor directly on the gen channel at --smoke (both arms, matched step, per-sub-task scores are already collected) before the bar is frozen.

findings.title: The SD the whole delta rests on is not stable enough to carry a threshold decision, and its upper CI refutes the delta's premise
findings.claim: sd_paired = 0.10138 is the mean of two SDs, each from 5 values (df=4). Its confidence interval spans the region where the delta is unnecessary and the region where the delta's premise is impossible.
findings.evidence: estimator_noise_anchor.py:182-196 computes per-rank cross-seed SDs (_estimator_noise_anchor.json: rank6 0.12705, rank8 0.07570) and averages them. Chi-square 95% CI at df=8: multipliers [0.6755, 1.9158] -> sd_paired in [0.0685, 0.1942]. The UPPER bound 0.1942 EXCEEDS the maximum possible pure-sampling paired SD at 24 items (0.14434 at p=0.5), so at the upper bound a trajectory floor is MANDATORY and the delta's central premise is false; the sampling model becomes impossible above 1.42x the point estimate. At the lower bound the noise is 0.68x and the delta is unnecessary. Single-observation leverage: dropping the 0.25 at seed 0 rank 6 (= 6 of 24 items) moves rank-6 SD 0.12705 -> 0.02082, sd_paired -> 0.04826, implied slope SE 0.02534 -> 0.01206 (1.67x bar -> 0.79x bar) at 24 items -- the headline of R5.5 turns on one datum. The anchor also averages SDs rather than pooling variances (0.10138 vs 0.10458), an undocumented estimator choice.
findings.fix: Publish the chi-square interval beside the point estimate, re-run the entire R5.6 operating-point table at both CI endpoints, and disclose the single-observation leverage. If the conclusion does not survive both endpoints, the measurement cannot license the threshold change.

findings.title: Channel transfer: the 2.16x precision projection credits two untested assumptions, and the gen channel has floor-producing mechanisms the T/F channel lacks
findings.claim: The 24 -> 112 item projection decomposes into sqrt(3/7)=1.53x from sub-task count and sqrt(8/16)=1.41x from items-per-sub-task. The second requires item-level independence inside a templated sub-task; the first is untestable at n=5. Neither is measured, and the gen channel adds two common-mode noise sources the anchor's model has no term for.
findings.evidence: GEN_DISJOINT_POOL sub-tasks are 16 near-identical templated items (e.g. ORTH_FIRST_GEN: 16 x 'Which of these words starts with the letter X'); a model that loses that skill loses them together. Exchangeable within-sub-task correlation rho, projected slope SE and sensitivity at bar 0.0076: rho=0 -> 0.01173 / 0.796; rho=0.05 -> 0.01336 / 0.714; rho=0.10 -> 0.01423 / 0.673; rho=0.20 -> 0.01515 / 0.630; rho=1.0 -> 0.01659 / 0.570. The 0.880 headline survives only at rho ~ 0. I tested the sub-task-independence leg on the cycle-43 data and it is uninformative: Var(aggregate delta) / (sub-task-independent prediction) = 1.90x at rank 6 (mean off-diagonal covariance +0.011458) and 0.26x at rank 8 (-0.023958) -- the only two available estimates differ by 7x and straddle 1.0 in opposite directions. Additionally the gen channel's repetition guard and echo guard ZERO items (capability_battery_gen.py constraints (b) and (c)), and guard fires are checkpoint-level common mode -- a checkpoint whose decodes collapse zeroes many items at once. That is precisely a trajectory floor, it has no term in the anchor's binomial model, and it cannot exist on the T/F margin channel the anchor measured.
findings.fix: Measure per-sub-task dispersion and guard-fire rates on the gen channel at --smoke and re-derive the projection from those. Do not credit the items-per-sub-task leg without a within-sub-task correlation estimate.

findings.title: The R5.6 operating-point table omits the outcome the delta exists to avoid; the 'plateau' is a plateau in one selected metric
findings.claim: The table reports sensitivity and false-positive only. Under a true null the halved bar destroys most of the design's ability to earn its own ANTICIPATED VERDICT and multiplies the PARTIAL rate -- the exact grievance R5.4 raised.
findings.evidence: My sim reproduces the R5.6 table exactly (112/0.0152 -> 0.499/0.002; 112/0.0076 -> 0.879/0.041; 48/0.0076 -> 0.622/0.063; 48/0.0152 -> 0.464/0.021), so the published columns are right. The omitted columns, frozen rule, 5 seeds, true slope 0: at 112 items, bar 0.0152 -> bounded_null 0.982 / PARTIAL 0.016; bar 0.0076 -> bounded_null 0.597 / PARTIAL 0.361. P(PARTIAL | true null) rises 22x. At 48 items (the realizable case per F2): 0.808/0.169 -> 0.285/0.653. PREREG:424-432 names the bounded null as the ANTICIPATED VERDICT and R5.4 objects that 'the most likely single outcome of the scored run is PARTIAL'. The claimed plateau ('0.845 to 0.886 across bar fractions 0.6 down to 0.33') is flat only in sensitivity; over the same range FPR runs 0.012 -> 0.055 and bounded-null-at-null runs 0.891 -> 0.420.
findings.fix: Publish the full four-column operating characteristic (sensitivity, FPR, bounded@null, PARTIAL@null) at every bar fraction and let the operator trade on it. Retract 'sits on a PLATEAU ... the provenance property that matters' or restate it over all four columns.

findings.title: FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX silently loosens by exactly the delta's claimed precision gain; sibling constants enumerated
findings.claim: The named hazard is CONFIRMED, and it is worse than stated: the format guard relaxes in lockstep with the sensitivity increase, and after the delta it admits a format-only shift larger than the entire price of interest.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v4.py:123 documents FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX = 0.0722 as 'one per-checkpoint aggregate SE at minimum selection'; it gates calibration at :1049 (`fi_selected['abs_delta'] <= FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX`) and the clean check at :1177. Measured in SE units: 48 items p=0.5 -> exactly 1.00 SE; 112 items p=0.5 -> 1.53 SE; 112 items p=0.90 -> 2.55 SE. The loosening factor 1.53x is identical to the claimed precision gain. As a multiple of the decision bar it goes 4.8x -> 9.5x; the receipt field `format_invariance_max_fraction_of_price` (:1062) goes 0.79x -> 1.58x of the price over the span, i.e. after the delta the guard admits a format-only aggregate shift LARGER than the price being measured. SIBLINGS: (i) INJECTION_GRID (:137) -- see F1, fatal; (ii) FI_DOSE_FRACTION (:114, used at :728 as `bar = FI_DOSE_FRACTION * MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE`) halves 0.0076 -> 0.0038, tightening the COUPLED->PARTIAL format demotion with an unpriced false-flag rate, and every spurious flag yields PARTIAL -- the outcome the delta exists to avoid; (iii) the --dry regression at :1533 asserting `est['mde80'] > MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE`, whose stated purpose (:1528) is to catch a bounded-null claim 'silently reverting to the 0.0152 bar' -- after the delta it only catches reversion to 0.0076 and can no longer detect a 0.0152 claim; (iv) SAFE, do not chase: FALSE_RECOVERY_ANALYTIC_MAX (:161) is a theorem about the frozen rule and is bar-invariant.
findings.fix: Re-derive FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX from the REALIZED selection size and realized clean aggregate rather than a frozen 48-item worst case. Add a --dry assertion that FORMAT_INVARIANCE_MAX / agg_SE and INJECTION_GRID[-1] * MIN_EFFECT_SLOPE are both recomputed from the run's own n_items and n_selected, so any future selection or bar change cannot move a guard silently.

findings.title: The projection rows discard the anchor's own measured accuracy and substitute the selection floor, inflating precision by 1.17x
findings.claim: estimator_noise_anchor.py section 7 measures the implied per-item accuracy, prints it, then does not use it for either gen projection row -- both assume p = 0.90, the CLEAN-base selection floor, for checkpoints that are mid-erasure.
findings.evidence: estimator_noise_anchor.py:261-263 hardcodes `("v4 gen battery, MIN_DISJOINT=3", 48, 0.90)` and `("v4 gen battery, all 7 disjoint", 112, 0.90)` while line 255 has just computed p_implied = 0.8559 from the data. _estimator_noise_anchor.json projection.rows confirms assumed_p 0.9 on both gen rows. At 112 items: p=0.90 -> slope SE 0.01002 (the quoted figure); the anchor's own p=0.8559 -> 0.01173; the REALIZED trained aggregate 0.8042 (measured by me from the same curves, ranks 6 and 8, both arms) -> 0.01326. Simply rescaling the measured SD with no p assumption at all, 0.10138 * sqrt(24/112) = 0.04693 -> 0.01173. The delta's quoted 0.0100 is optimistic by 1.17x against its own measurement. Sensitivity at bar 0.0076 falls 0.879 -> 0.796 on that correction alone, before any of F3/F5 is applied.
findings.fix: Use the measured SD rescaled by item count directly -- it needs no p at all. If a p-parameterised row is kept, use the realized trained accuracy, not the clean-base selection floor, and say which is which on the receipt.

findings.title: The p=0.856 vs p=0.144 root choice is immaterial to the arithmetic but its stated justification is wrong
findings.claim: Lens (c): the low root is correctly excluded, the choice does not change any number, but the reason given in the code is factually incorrect and the same data that exclude the low root also sit below the high root.
findings.evidence: estimator_noise_anchor.py:253-255: tgt = p(1-p) = 0.123336, roots 0.8559 and 0.1441; the high root is taken with the parenthetical justification 'take the high root; the battery floor is 0.90'. Since p(1-p) is symmetric about 0.5, both roots give the identical sampling SD and the identical projection -- the choice is cosmetic for the arithmetic. The justification is wrong on its own terms: 0.90 is DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN, a selection floor on the CLEAN base (coupling_confirm_v4.py:103), not a floor on trained checkpoints; the realized trained aggregate over the fitted ranks is 0.8042, which is below 0.90 and also below 0.8559.
findings.fix: Replace the comment with the actual exclusion (realized aggregate 0.804 rules out 0.144), state that the root choice does not affect the projection, and note that the same measurement sits 0.05 below the high root.
